abstract: Comprehensive Laravel 12 Lucid Architecture guide designed for AI agents and LLMs. Contains 14 rules across 7 categories, prioritized by impact from critical (foundation and feature patterns) to incremental (advanced patterns). Each rule includes detailed explanations, real-world examples with bad and good implementations using PHP 8.3 and Laravel 12 features, and specific impact metrics to guide automated refactoring and code generation. Focuses on Lucid Architecture patterns: domains as business logic boundaries, features as user-facing entry points, operations as orchestrators, and jobs as atomic units of work.

categories.name: Foundation & Structure
categories.description: Core Lucid concepts: directory layout, services, domains, features, operations, and jobs

categories.name: Features & Operations
categories.description: Feature classes as entry points and operations as orchestrators of jobs

categories.name: Domains & Services
categories.description: Domain boundaries, models, cross-domain communication, and service isolation

categories.name: Jobs & Units
categories.description: Atomic job design, dependency injection, reusability, and queue integration

categories.name: Data & Validation
categories.description: Data objects, transformers, validation rules, and attribute casting

categories.name: Testing
categories.description: Testing features, operations, and jobs at appropriate levels of isolation

categories.name: Advanced Patterns
categories.description: Multi-service monolith, event-driven architecture, and API versioning
